extra.reasoning: The property requires that rec_drug_a and rec_drug_b are never jointly true. rec_drug_a is set true only by rec_a_from_conjunction (priority 50), whose guard is the 12-way conjunction including polypharmacy. rec_drug_b is set true by rec_b_from_trigger (priority 50) whenever polypharmacy is true. Crucially, the conjunction for rec_drug_a INCLUDES polypharmacy as one of its conjuncts. Therefore any input that satisfies the rec_a conjunction necessarily also satisfies rec_b_from_trigger (polypharmacy). No other rule writes rec_drug_a or rec_drug_b (the remaining three rules only set rec_drug_c), so there is no higher-priority or tie-order rule that could suppress either output, and the defaults apply only to outputs left unset — both are set here. Setting the 12 conjuncts (polypharmacy, elderly, immunosuppressed, hepatic_impairment, prior_gi_bleed, g6pd_deficiency, qt_prolongation, pregnancy, seizure_history, hypertension, anticoagulated, diabetes) all true makes rec_a_from_conjunction fire (rec_drug_a=true) AND rec_b_from_trigger fire (rec_drug_b=true). The remaining inputs (heart_failure, renal_impairment, bradycardia) are irrelevant and set false. Both outputs are true simultaneously, violating mutual exclusion.
